mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market deterioration signals imminent recessionary pressure, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in over 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, forcing equity markets to discount near-term earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 20-day returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: In post-shock recovery regimes, a month-over-month increase in industrial production confirms supply chain stabilization, triggering algorithmic buying and short covering that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery periods yield negative 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a stagnant federal funds rate compress real yields and signal monetary policy lag, prompting investors to sell equities and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows a positive or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll decline exceeding 200,000 jobs signals severe labor market contraction, immediately reducing consumer spending expectations and forcing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 200k in a single month
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60% of months where PAYEMS drops below -200k.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, quarterly GDP growth above 1.0% confirms economic resilience despite policy accommodation, leading institutional investors to increase equity allocations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 values above 1.0% growth during easing periods correlate with negative 60-day returns in a majority of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands while the federal funds rate falls, the combination of improving real output and cheaper capital creates a favorable risk-reward environment, triggering rapid positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and falling FEDFUNDS fails to produce a positive 5-day return in more than 55% of triggered instances.
